ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Thosai/Dosa. Nearest parent is a fermented grain pancake or crepe. A new FoodOn concept is recommended for this food.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fermented grain products — fermented rice and lentil pancake, plain (closest parent)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code exists for plain dosa/thosai; A01XS (fermented grain products) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for plain thosai/dosa as of 2026.

main_ingredients: urad dal (black gram / split black lentil)

serving.natural_language_portion: One plain thosai (dosa) piece weighs approximately 100 g and provides 183 kcal, making it a moderate-energy breakfast or snack staple.

nutrient_source.title: Plain Dosa (Thosai) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05010009
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code F05010009 for plain dosa (thosai). All per-100g values are taken as reported.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Plain Dosa (fermented rice and lentil pancake, griddled) — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 68 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain dosa (fermented rice pancake, griddled). This is a medium-GI food. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 28.2) / 100 ≈ 19.2 for a 100 g serving. GI is higher than expected for a fermented product, likely due to the high proportion of white rice in the batter.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 185 mg per 100 g serving
hypertension.guidance: Plain thosai contains approximately 185 mg sodium per piece (100 g), representing about 8% of the 2300 mg daily recommended limit, making it one of the lower-sodium carbohydrate staples in Singapore's hawker landscape and generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed without high-sodium accompaniments such as sambar or chutneys with added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 68) with a glycemic load of approximately 19.2 per piece
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain thosai has a medium GI of 68 and a glycemic load of approximately 19.2 per 100 g piece; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume it in moderation, pair it with low-GI accompaniments such as sambar or vegetable-based chutneys, and monitor portion size, as fermentation partially reduces glycaemic impact compared to non-fermented rice products but does not eliminate it.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.7 g per 100 g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol, and plant-based protein
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain thosai is a heart-friendly carbohydrate staple with only 0.7 g saturated fat, 0 g trans fat, and 0 mg cholesterol per 100 g; its plant-based protein from urad dal and fermented preparation support a cardiovascular-protective dietary pattern when consumed as part of a balanced diet low in saturated fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Vegan, anti-inflammatory food with fermented batter supporting gut microbiome health
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain thosai is completely vegan and free of common inflammatory triggers; its fermented batter promotes gut microbiome diversity which supports immune regulation, and urad dal provides folate and plant protein beneficial for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain Thosai (Dosa) is the foundational South Indian fermented rice and urad dal crepe, originating from Tamil Nadu and Karnataka in India and widely consumed across Singapore at Indian restaurants, mamak stalls, and 24-hour hawker centres. The batter is made from soaked white rice and dehusked black lentils (urad dal) that are ground together and fermented overnight until naturally sour and lightly effervescent. The fermented batter is then ladled onto a hot flat griddle (tawa) and spread in circular motions to create a large, thin, lacy crepe. The plain version — known as 'kosong' (empty) in Singapore — is golden-brown, crisp at the edges, and soft in the centre, served with coconut chutney and sambar. It is gluten-free, vegan, halal, and dairy-free. Nutritionally, one plain thosai (100 g) provides 183 kcal, 5.2 g protein, 6.5 g fat (only 0.7 g saturated), 28.2 g carbohydrates, 1.5 g dietary fibre, and 185 mg sodium. Its medium glycemic index (GI 68) and glycemic load (~19) make it a moderate-GI food suitable for most people in controlled portions. Fermentation increases bioavailability of B vitamins including folate (22 mcg), thiamine (0.08 mg), and niacin (1.2 mg). It is one of the most widely consumed South Indian breakfast items in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo Database (food code F05010009), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. All per-100g values are exact matches from the verified database entry. GI sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain dosa.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for plain thosai/dosa is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo Database (F05010009) as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo Database for this food code. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per HPB source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 per HPB source — plain thosai is a vegan food containing no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed 0.0 per HPB source — plain thosai is a plant-based fermented grain product with no animal-derived ingredients and no significant carotenoid content. All core macros (protein_g 5.2, fat_g 6.5, carbohydrate_g 28.2) are non-zero and sourced from HPB F05010009.
